question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a bulk rewrite of all `index` values in the `record_file` table by injecting a crafted v6 RecordFile whose `index` field is set to an arbitrary value, causing `RecordFileParser.updateIndex()` to call `repository.updateIndex(offset)` with an attacker-controlled offset, thereby renumbering every historical block and breaking block-number-based transaction lookups?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged user cause `updateIndex()` to apply a negative offset to all stored `index` values by supplying a v6 RecordFile with an `index` lower than the last v5 file's `index`, resulting in block numbers becoming negative or colliding and causing APIs to return transactions under wrong block numbers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setIndex()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a manipulated `index` field that is identical to an already-persisted block's `index`, violating the unique index constraint on `record_file(index)` and causing the mirror node to reject or skip legitimate subsequent blocks, effectively creating a gap in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setPreviousHash()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordFile whose `previousHash` field is set to the all-zeros empty hash (matching `DigestAlgorithm.SHA_384.emptyHash`), exploiting the `verifyHashChain()` bypass that skips validation when `expectedPreviousHash` is empty, to inject a file that breaks the hash chain continuity and allows reordering of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setHash()] Can an unprivileged user inject a RecordFile with a forged `hash` field that matches the `previousHash` of a future file already in the pipeline, causing the hash chain to accept an out-of-order file and silently reorder the sequence of persisted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setConsensusEnd()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a `consensusEnd` value that is less than or equal to the `consensusStart` of the same file, bypassing the `shouldParse()` overlap check (`previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()`) and causing the parser to skip the file silently, creating a gap in the persisted transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setConsensusStart()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordFile with a `consensusStart` value that overlaps with the `consensusEnd` of the previously persisted file, triggering the `shouldParse()` skip condition and causing the mirror node to silently drop a legitimate file, creating a permanent gap in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setVersion()] Can an unprivileged user manipulate the `version` field of a RecordFile to a value below `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION` (6) when the actual content is v6, causing `setStreamFileIndex()` to incorrectly assign a sequential `last+1` index instead of the canonical block number, thereby misaligning all subsequent block numbers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setVersion()] Can an unprivileged user set `version` to a value >= `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION` (6) on a pre-v6 file, bypassing the `setStreamFileIndex()` call that would assign the correct sequential index, causing the file to be stored with an uninitialized or zero `index` and disrupting block number ordering?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEmpty()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the identity-based `isEmpty()` check (`this == EMPTY`) by constructing a RecordFile with all fields matching `RecordFile.EMPTY` but as a distinct object instance, causing downstream code that relies on `isEmpty()` to incorrectly process an empty file as a real one, inserting a phantom block into transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where `copy()` (via `toBuilder().build()`) produces a shallow copy that shares mutable `byte[]` references (`bytes`, `logsBloom`, `previousWrappedRecordBlockHash`, `wrappedRecordBlockHash`) with the original, allowing mutation of the cached `lastStreamFile` copy after it is stored, corrupting the hash chain reference used for the next file's validation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: clear()] Can an unprivileged user cause `clear()` to be called on a RecordFile before its `hash` and `previousHash` are persisted to the database, by triggering a concurrent parse operation, resulting in null hash values being stored and breaking the hash chain for all subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with `hapiVersionMajor`, `hapiVersionMinor`, or `hapiVersionPatch` set to null, causing `hapiVersion()` to return `HAPI_VERSION_NOT_SET` (0.0.0), which then causes version-gated parsing logic to apply incorrect deserialization paths to transaction records, silently corrupting the order or content of parsed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordFile with `hapiVersionMajor=0`, `hapiVersionMinor=0`, `hapiVersionPatch=0` (matching `HAPI_VERSION_NOT_SET`) to force the lazy `hapiVersion` field to return the sentinel version, bypassing version-specific transaction parsing branches and causing transactions to be parsed under the wrong protocol version, reordering or misattributing historical records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setCount()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a `count` field set to zero or a negative value while `items` contains actual transactions, causing metrics and downstream consumers that rely on `count` to believe the block is empty, effectively hiding transactions from block explorers without removing them from the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setCount()] Can an unprivileged user set `count` to an arbitrarily large value on a RecordFile with few actual items, causing APIs that use `count` to report inflated transaction counts per block, misleading block explorers and analytics tools about the true transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setName()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a `name` field that does not match the expected timestamp-based filename format, causing `StreamFilename.getTimestamp()` to return an incorrect `loadStart` value, which then corrupts the ordering of files in the download queue and causes files to be processed out of chronological order?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setName()] Can an unprivileged user inject a RecordFile with a `name` that duplicates an already-processed file's name, causing the mirror node to treat it as a new file and attempt to re-parse it, potentially inserting duplicate transaction records or overwriting existing ones and disrupting the sequential transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setFileHash()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a `fileHash` that does not match the actual SHA-384 digest of the file bytes, bypassing the `verifyHash()` check if the corresponding signature file is also forged, causing an unverified file to be accepted and its transactions inserted into history in the wrong order?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setMetadataHash()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a null or empty `metadataHash` (which is `@Transient` and not persisted), causing the `verifyHash()` call for metadata hash to pass vacuously when the expected hash is also null, allowing a file with tampered metadata to be accepted and its block ordering to be accepted without full verification?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordFile with a null `previousWrappedRecordBlockHash`, exploiting the null check in `BlockStreamVerifier.verifyHashChain()` (`if (previousWrappedRecordBlockHash != null)`) to bypass the wrapped record block hash chain verification, allowing a block to be inserted without validating its continuity with the previous wrapped block?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setWrappedRecordBlockHash()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with a forged `wrappedRecordBlockHash` that matches the `previousWrappedRecordBlockHash` of a future block, causing the wrapped block hash chain to accept an out-of-order block and silently reorder the sequence of wrapped record blocks in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setIndex()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with `index` set to `Long.MAX_VALUE` or `Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing integer overflow in `updateIndex()` offset calculation (`recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1`) and triggering a bulk update that sets all block indices to overflow values, completely corrupting the block number sequence?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setRoundStart() / setRoundEnd()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with `roundStart` greater than `roundEnd`, or with round values that do not correspond to the actual consensus round, causing APIs that expose round-based transaction queries to return transactions in incorrect round order, effectively reorganizing the visible transaction history by round?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setGasUsed()] Can an unprivileged user supply a RecordFile with `gasUsed` set to `-1` (the sentinel value used by `findLatestMissingGasUsedBefore()`), causing the gas backfill query to incorrectly identify this file as missing gas data and trigger a re-processing pass that overwrites the correct gas values of adjacent blocks?
